serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Minute Maid Orange Juice is one 300ml bottle or carton.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central - Orange juice, chilled, includes from concentrate
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central for 'Orange juice, chilled, includes from concentrate' as a close match for commercially prepared Minute Maid Orange Juice. Original input values were used where available and plausible, otherwise USDA values were used.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database - Orange juice, commercial, unsweetened
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI for commercial, unsweetened orange juice (50) used as an analogous estimate. While Minute Maid may contain added sugar, this provides a reasonable baseline for the fruit juice component. The GL per serving is calculated based on the carbohydrate content of this record and a 300g serving size.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content, particularly fructose, can contribute to increased blood pressure.
hypertension.guidance: Limit consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages like orange juice, as high sugar intake is independently linked to hypertension risk, regardless of sodium content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (8.4g/100g) and low fiber lead to rapid glucose absorption and blood sugar spikes.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Avoid or strictly limit intake of fruit juices, even those without added sugar, due to their high concentration of free sugars and lack of fiber, which can negatively impact blood glucose control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High intake of added sugars, especially from beverages, is associated with increased risk of cardiovascular disease.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Reduce consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages to lower the risk of cardiovascular disease, opting for water or unsweetened beverages instead.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Minute Maid Orange Juice is a commercially prepared and bottled beverage made primarily from orange juice concentrate, water, and often added sugar. It is a popular, refreshing drink with a sweet and tangy flavor profile. Nutritionally, it is a source of Vitamin C but is also high in sugars with very little dietary fiber. Due to its high sugar content and rapid absorption, it is generally not recommended for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension. It is typically consumed as a standalone beverage or as part of a meal. This product is suitable for vegetarian, vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free diets.
common_user_queries: Is Minute Maid Orange Juice healthy?
Is orange juice good for diabetics?
Ingredients in Minute Maid Orange Juice

confidence_reason: Nutrient data is adapted from USDA FoodData Central for a generic commercial orange juice, as exact Minute Maid product data was not available from approved sources. GI is an analogous estimate. Health suitability is based on general guidelines for fruit juices and sugar-sweetened beverages.
notes: Protein_g, fat_g, saturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are confirmed zero or trace amounts per USDA FoodData Central for orange juice. Dietary fibre_g is very low, as expected for juice without pulp. Sodium_mg is naturally low. Vitamin_c_mg is a significant nutrient in orange juice. The input data for per_100g was partially used, but supplemented and adjusted based on USDA FoodData Central for better accuracy and completeness.
